ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT YARWAY CORPORATION'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY ACTION (FORUM NON CONVENIENS) IS GRANTED AND ITS MOTION TO DISMISS IS DENIED. THE CASE IS STAYED PENDING A DETERMINATION WHETHER ALL DEFENDANTS ARE AMENABLE TO JURISDICTION IN MONTANA OR WASHINGTON. THE DECEDENT'S EXTENSIVE EXPOSURE HISTORY IN THOSE STATES COMPARED TO HIS MINIMAL EXPOSURE HISTORY IN CALIFORNIA STRONGLY FAVORS HAVING THIS CASE DECIDED IN MONTANA OR WASHINGTON IF ALL THE DEFENDANTS ARE AMENABLE TO JURISDICTION THERE. YARWAY'S FAILURE TO ESTABLISH THAT ALL DEFENDANTS ARE AMENABLE TO JURISDICTION IN MONTANA OR WASHINGTON REQUIRES DENIAL OF ITS MOTION TO DISMISS. (SEE REPORTER'S NOTES FOR FURTHER DETAILS). PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

Defendant Yarway Corporations Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action (Forum Non Conveniens)
Off Calendar Apr-29-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Graham Mills, Esq. of MORGAN LEWIS (415-442-1227), appearing for defendant / moving party Yarway Corporation
Paula Rasmussen, Esq.of BRENT COON & ASSOCIATES (415-489-7420), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Shirley Erickson, et al.

RULING:
Defendant Yarway Corporation's motion to dismiss or stay action. Matter was argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion to stay Action (Forum Non Conveniens) is granted and its motion to dismiss is denied. The case is stayed pending a determination whether all defendants are amenable to jurisdiction in Montana or Washington. The decedent's extensive exposure history in those states compared to his minimal exposure history in California strongly favors having this case decided in Montana or Washington if all the defendants are amenable to jurisdiction there. Yarway's failure to establish that all defendants are amenable to jurisdiction in Montana or Washington requires denial of its motion to dismiss. (See reporter's notes for further details). Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
